title: lac export-all: batch all export formats to a folder
problem: Running each export format individually requires 15+ separate CLI invocations. Users who want a full snapshot of their workspace — for sharing, archiving, or CI artifacts — have no single command to produce all outputs at once.
analysis: The export-all command is a batch runner over the existing generator library. It scans features once from a given directory, then calls every multi-feature generator (14 HTML formats + prompt + site) and writes outputs to a single folder. The architecture is a thin registry pattern: an array of `{name, file, generate}` records drives the main loop, keeping the command extensible without code duplication. Non-applicable single-feature formats (postcard, markdown) and comparison formats (diff) are intentionally excluded. The site generator writes a directory tree rather than a single file, so it is handled separately with its own mkdir. A `--skip` flag accepts comma-separated format names so users can exclude slow or unwanted outputs. Errors per format are non-fatal — the loop continues and the summary reports failures.

decisions.decision: Registry array drives the loop rather than hardcoded if-chains
decisions.rationale: Adding a new export format in the future requires one entry in the HTML_FORMATS array, not a new if-block. Mirrors the generator library's own open/closed structure.
alternativesConsidered: Individual if-blocks per format (copied from export.ts)
Dynamic import via format name string

decisions.decision: Scan features once, pass to all generators
decisions.rationale: Avoids repeated filesystem walks. All generators accept the same ScannedFeature[] type, so one scan suffices for all 14+ formats.
alternativesConsidered: Re-scan per format (simpler but slow for large workspaces)

decisions.decision: Exclude postcard, markdown, and diff from export-all
decisions.rationale: postcard and markdown are single-feature formats that require a nearest feature.json context. diff requires a second workspace path. None are meaningful in a batch context.
alternativesConsidered: Include postcard using first frozen feature

implementation: HTML_FORMATS registry: array of {name, file, generate} where generate wraps each generator with the correct argument shape. ALL_FORMAT_NAMES: used for --skip validation. Action: resolves scanDir and outDir, validates skip names, scans features, mkdir outDir, iterates HTML_FORMATS (skipping or writing), handles prompt (buildReconstructionPrompt from export.ts, writes lac-prompt.md), handles site (generateSite to outDir/site/). Prints ✓/✗/skip per file. Summary line: N exported, M failed, K skipped.
knownLimitations: No --view or --tags support — export-all targets full snapshots, not filtered views
Site output is always written to <outDir>/site/ with no configurable subdirectory name
Generators that fail do not abort the run — partial output is possible without a clear error exit code
Does not support postcard or markdown (single-feature only) or diff (requires a second target)
successCriteria: `lac export-all` with 3 features produces 16 files (14 html + lac-prompt.md + site/) in ./lac-exports/. `--skip site,quiz` produces 14 files. `--skip unknownformat` exits with an error message listing valid formats. Each generated file is non-empty.
userGuide: Run `lac export-all` in any LAC workspace to generate all export formats at once. Outputs go to `./lac-exports/` by default. Use `--out <dir>` to change the destination. Use `--skip <formats>` to exclude specific formats (e.g. `--skip site,quiz` if you don't need the static site). Pass a path argument to scan a specific directory instead of cwd.

publicInterface.name: exportAllCommand
publicInterface.description: Commander.js command exported and registered in src/index.ts as 'export-all'.
